content:
# Exodus Chapter 40 — The EMET Bible (Literal)

**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), pp. 112–113; Codex Vaticanus (Vat.gr.1209), pp. 83–84 (LXX) | **Source Images:** `source-texts/hebrew-OT/EXO_040_page_001.jpg`, `EXO_040_page_002.jpg`; `source-texts/greek-OT-LXX/EXO_040_vaticanus_page_001.jpg`, `EXO_040_vaticanus_page_002.jpg`

---

> **MANUSCRIPT OBSERVATION — ERECTION OF THE TABERNACLE AND THE GLORY OF YHWH:** The Leningrad Codex presents Exodus 40, the final chapter of the book, across pages 112–113. This chapter contains YHWH's command to erect the tabernacle (vv. 1–16), Moses's obedient execution of each instruction (vv. 17–33), and the climactic descent of the glory-cloud upon the tent of meeting (vv. 34–38). The refrain "as YHWH commanded Moses" (ka'asher tsivvah YHWH et-Mosheh) appears eight times in vv. 19–33, continuing the sevenfold-plus pattern from ch. 39 and binding the entire construction narrative to divine instruction. The chapter closes with the cloud-and-fire theology that will govern Israel's wilderness journeys — the visible presence (kavod) of YHWH filling the mishkan. The Vaticanus LXX (pp. 83–84, heading CAP. XXXIX visible at top of p. 83, as LXX numbers this chapter 40 differently in some traditions) follows the MT structure closely for this chapter, unlike the significant reordering seen in chs. 35–39. Minor LXX expansions appear, but the verse sequence and content align well with the Hebrew Vorlage.

---

## YHWH Commands the Erection of the Tabernacle (vv. 1–8)

**1** And YHWH spoke [va-yedabber] unto [el] Moses [Mosheh] saying [le'mor]:

> **Key terms:** וַיְדַבֵּר (va-yedabber) = "and he spoke" — the standard divine-speech formula opening the final chapter. **LXX:** καὶ ἐλάλησεν κύριος ("and the Lord spoke").

**2** "On the day of [be-yom] the first [ha-rishon] month [khodesh], on the first [be-ekhad] of the month [la-khodesh], you shall raise up [taqim] the tabernacle of [et-mishkan] the tent of meeting [ohel mo'ed]."

> **Key terms:** תָּקִים (taqim) = "you shall raise up/erect" — from קום (hiphil); the pivotal verb of the chapter. מִשְׁכַּן אֹהֶל מוֹעֵד (mishkan ohel mo'ed) = "tabernacle of the tent of meeting" — the full double designation. **LXX:** στήσεις τὴν σκηνήν ("you shall set up the tent").

**3** "And you shall place [ve-samta] there [sham] the ark of [et-aron] the testimony [ha-edut], and you shall screen [ve-sakkota] over [al] the ark [ha-aron] with the curtain [et-ha-parokhet]."

> **Key terms:** פָּרֹכֶת (parokhet) = "curtain/veil" — the dividing screen between the holy place and the most holy place. וְסַכֹּתָ (ve-sakkota) = "and you shall screen/cover" — from סכך ("to cover/shield"). **LXX:** καὶ σκεπάσεις ("and you shall cover").

**4** "And you shall bring in [ve-heve'ta] the table [et-ha-shulkhan] and you shall arrange [ve-arakhta] its arrangement [et-erkho], and you shall bring in [ve-heve'ta] the lampstand [et-ha-menorah] and you shall set up [ve-ha'aleta] its lamps [et-neroteiha]."

> **Key terms:** עֶרְכּוֹ (erkho) = "its arrangement" — the rows of showbread. וְהַעֲלֵתָ (ve-ha'aleta) = "and you shall cause to go up" — hiphil of עלה; lamps are "raised" (lit. kindled). **LXX:** ἐπιθήσεις τοὺς λύχνους ("you shall place the lamps").

**5** "And you shall set [ve-natatta] the altar of [et-mizbach] the gold [ha-zahav] for the incense [la-qetoret] before [lifnei] the ark of [aron] the testimony [ha-edut], and you shall set [ve-samta] the screen of [et-masakh] the entrance [ha-petakh] for the tabernacle [la-mishkan]."

> **Key terms:** קְטֹרֶת (qetoret) = "incense" — from קטר ("to burn/make smoke"). מָסָךְ (masakh) = "screen/hanging" — the outer screen at the tabernacle entrance, distinct from the inner parokhet. **LXX:** τὸ θυσιαστήριον τὸ χρυσοῦν ("the golden altar").

**6** "And you shall set [ve-natatta] the altar of [et-mizbach] the burnt offering [ha-olah] before [lifnei] the entrance of [petakh] the tabernacle of [mishkan] the tent of meeting [ohel mo'ed]."

> **Key terms:** עֹלָה (olah) = "burnt offering" — from עלה ("to go up"); the whole-ascension offering. **LXX:** τὸ θυσιαστήριον τῶν ὁλοκαυτωμάτων ("the altar of whole-burnt-offerings").

**7** "And you shall set [ve-natatta] the basin [et-ha-kiyyor] between [bein] the tent of meeting [ohel mo'ed] and between [u-vein] the altar [ha-mizbeakh], and you shall put [ve-natatta] there [sham] water [mayim]."

> **Key terms:** כִּיּוֹר (kiyyor) = "basin/laver" — the bronze washing basin for priestly purification. **LXX:** τὸν λουτῆρα ("the laver").

**8** "And you shall set [ve-samta] the courtyard [et-he-khatser] all around [saviv], and you shall set [ve-natatta] the screen of [et-masakh] the gate of [sha'ar] the courtyard [he-khatser]."

> **Key terms:** חָצֵר (khatser) = "courtyard/enclosure" — the outer perimeter of the sacred space. שַׁעַר (sha'ar) = "gate" — the single entrance on the east side. **LXX:** τὴν αὐλήν ("the courtyard").

---

## Anointing and Consecration Commands (vv. 9–16)

**9** "And you shall take [ve-laqakhta] the oil of [et-shemen] the anointing [ha-mishkhah] and you shall anoint [u-mashakhta] the tabernacle [et-ha-mishkan] and all that [ve-et-kol-asher] is in it [bo], and you shall consecrate [ve-qiddashta] it [oto] and all [ve-et-kol] its vessels [kelav], and it shall be [ve-hayah] holy [qodesh]."

> **Key terms:** שֶׁמֶן הַמִּשְׁחָה (shemen ha-mishkhah) = "oil of the anointing" — the specially compounded sacred oil (30:22–33). וְקִדַּשְׁתָּ (ve-qiddashta) = "and you shall consecrate/make holy" — piel of קדש. **LXX:** χρίσεις ("you shall anoint") ... ἁγιάσεις ("you shall sanctify").

**10** "And you shall anoint [u-mashakhta] the altar of [et-mizbach] the burnt offering [ha-olah] and all [ve-et-kol] its vessels [kelav], and you shall consecrate [ve-qiddashta] the altar [et-ha-mizbeakh], and the altar shall be [ve-hayah ha-mizbeakh] a holy-of-holies [qodesh qodashim]."

> **Key terms:** קֹדֶשׁ קָדָשִׁים (qodesh qodashim) = "a holy-of-holies" — the superlative; anything touching the altar becomes consecrated. **LXX:** ἅγιον τῶν ἁγίων ("holy of holies").

**11** "And you shall anoint [u-mashakhta] the basin [et-ha-kiyyor] and its stand [ve-et-kanno], and you shall consecrate [ve-qiddashta] it [oto]."

> **Key terms:** כַּנּוֹ (kanno) = "its stand/base" — the pedestal of the bronze basin. **LXX:** τὴν βάσιν αὐτοῦ ("its base").

**12** "And you shall bring near [ve-hiqravta] Aaron [et-Aharon] and his sons [ve-et-banav] unto [el] the entrance of [petakh] the tent of meeting [ohel mo'ed], and you shall wash [ve-rakhatsta] them [otam] with water [ba-mayim]."

> **Key terms:** וְהִקְרַבְתָּ (ve-hiqravta) = "and you shall bring near/present" — hiphil of קרב; priestly approach to the sacred. וְרָחַצְתָּ (ve-rakhatsta) = "and you shall wash" — ritual washing preceding investiture. **LXX:** προσάξεις ("you shall bring forward").

**13** "And you shall clothe [ve-hilbashta] Aaron [et-Aharon] with the garments of [et-bigdei] the holy [ha-qodesh], and you shall anoint [u-mashakhta] him [oto] and you shall consecrate [ve-qiddashta] him [oto], and he shall serve-as-priest [ve-khihen] to me [li]."

> **Key terms:** וְכִהֵן (ve-khihen) = "and he shall serve-as-priest" — piel denominative from כֹּהֵן; the purpose of anointing. **LXX:** ἱερατεύσει μοι ("he shall be priest to me").

**14** "And his sons [ve-et-banav] you shall bring near [taqriv], and you shall clothe [ve-hilbashta] them [otam] with tunics [kutanot]."

> **Key terms:** כֻּתֳּנֹת (kutanot) = "tunics" — the basic linen garments of the ordinary priests. **LXX:** χιτῶνας ("tunics").

**15** "And you shall anoint [u-mashakhta] them [otam] as [ka'asher] you anointed [mashakhta] their father [et-avihem], and they shall serve-as-priests [ve-khihanu] to me [li]. And it shall be [ve-hayetah] — their anointing [mishkhatam] shall be [tihyeh] for them [lahem] for an eternal [le-olam] priesthood [kehunnat] throughout their generations [le-dorotam]."

> **Key terms:** כְּהֻנַּת עוֹלָם (kehunnat olam) = "an eternal priesthood" — the perpetual Aaronic mandate. לְדֹרֹתָם (le-dorotam) = "throughout their generations." **LXX:** εἰς τὸν αἰῶνα ("unto the age").

**16** And Moses did [va-ya'as Mosheh]; according to all [ke-khol] that [asher] YHWH commanded [tsivvah] him [oto], so [ken] he did [asah].

> **Key terms:** כֵּן עָשָׂה (ken asah) = "so he did" — the summary affirmation of Moses's total obedience. **LXX:** ἐποίησεν ("he did") — Vaticanus faithfully renders the obedience formula.

---

## Moses Erects the Tabernacle (vv. 17–33)

**17** And it was [va-yehi] in the first [ha-rishon] month [ba-khodesh], in the second [ba-shanah ha-shenit] year [shanah], on the first [be-ekhad] of the month [la-khodesh], the tabernacle was raised up [huqam ha-mishkan].

> **Key terms:** הוּקַם (huqam) = "was raised up" — hophal passive of קום; the tabernacle is erected. בַּשָּׁנָה הַשֵּׁנִית (ba-shanah ha-shenit) = "in the second year" — one year after the exodus. **LXX:** ἐστάθη ἡ σκηνή ("the tent was set up").

**18** And Moses raised up [va-yaqem Mosheh] the tabernacle [et-ha-mishkan] and he set [va-yitten] its bases [et-adanav] and he placed [va-yasem] its frames [et-qerashaiv] and he set [va-yitten] its bars [et-berikhav] and he raised up [va-yaqem] its pillars [et-ammudav].

> **Key terms:** אֲדָנִים (adanim) = "bases/sockets" — silver foundation sockets. קְרָשִׁים (qerashim) = "frames/boards" — the structural wooden frames. **LXX:** τὰς κεφαλίδας ("the capitals") for bars; otherwise close.

**19** And he spread [va-yifros] the tent [et-ha-ohel] over [al] the tabernacle [ha-mishkan], and he placed [va-yasem] the covering of [et-mikhseh] the tent [ha-ohel] upon it [alav] from above [mi-lma'elah], as [ka'asher] YHWH commanded [tsivvah] Moses [et-Mosheh].

> **Key terms:** מִכְסֶה (mikhseh) = "covering" — the outer layers of ram-skin and takhash-skin over the tent-cloth. מִלְמָעְלָה (mi-lma'elah) = "from above." **LXX:** τὸ κατακάλυμμα τῆς σκηνῆς ("the covering of the tent").

**20** And he took [va-yiqqakh] and he set [va-yitten] the testimony [et-ha-edut] unto [el] the ark [ha-aron], and he placed [va-yasem] the poles [et-ha-baddim] upon [al] the ark [ha-aron], and he set [va-yitten] the mercy-seat [et-ha-kapporet] upon [al] the ark [ha-aron] from above [mi-lma'elah].

> **Key terms:** עֵדֻת (edut) = "testimony" — the stone tablets placed inside the ark. כַּפֹּרֶת (kapporet) = "mercy-seat/atonement-cover" — from כפר ("to atone/cover"); the gold lid with cherubim. **LXX:** τὸ ἱλαστήριον ("the propitiatory/mercy-seat").

**21** And he brought [va-yave'] the ark [et-ha-aron] into [el] the tabernacle [ha-mishkan], and he set [va-yasem] the curtain of [et parokhet] the screen [ha-masakh], and he screened [va-yasekh] over [al] the ark of [aron] the testimony [ha-edut], as [ka'asher] YHWH commanded [tsivvah] Moses [et-Mosheh].

> **Key terms:** וַיָּסֶךְ (va-yasekh) = "and he screened" — from סכך; the curtain shields the ark from view. פָּרֹכֶת הַמָּסָךְ (parokhet ha-masakh) = "curtain of the screen." **LXX:** τὸ καταπέτασμα τοῦ καλύμματος ("the veil of the covering").

**22** And he set [va-yitten] the table [et-ha-shulkhan] in the tent of [be-ohel] meeting [mo'ed], on the side of [al yerekh] the tabernacle [ha-mishkan] northward [tsafonah], outside [mi-khuts] the curtain [la-parokhet].

> **Key terms:** יֶרֶךְ (yerekh) = "side/flank" — literally "thigh." צָפוֹנָה (tsafonah) = "northward." **LXX:** ἐπὶ τὸ κλίτος... πρὸς βορρᾶν ("on the side... toward the north").

**23** And he arranged [va-ya'arokh] upon it [alav] an arrangement of [erekh] bread [lekhem] before [lifnei] YHWH, as [ka'asher] YHWH commanded [tsivvah] Moses [et-Mosheh].

> **Key terms:** עֵרֶךְ לֶחֶם (erekh lekhem) = "arrangement of bread" — the showbread/bread of the Presence (לֶחֶם הַפָּנִים). **LXX:** προθέσεως ("of setting forth") — ἄρτους ("breads").

**24** And he placed [va-yasem] the lampstand [et-ha-menorah] in the tent of [be-ohel] meeting [mo'ed], opposite [nokhakh] the table [ha-shulkhan], on the side of [al yerekh] the tabernacle [ha-mishkan] southward [negbah].

> **Key terms:** נֹכַח (nokhakh) = "opposite/in front of" — the lampstand and table face each other across the holy place. נֶגְבָּה (negbah) = "southward." **LXX:** ἀπέναντι τῆς τραπέζης ("opposite the table").

**25** And he set up [va-ya'al] the lamps [ha-nerot] before [lifnei] YHWH, as [ka'asher] YHWH commanded [tsivvah] Moses [et-Mosheh].

> **Key terms:** וַיַּעַל (va-ya'al) = "and he caused to go up" — hiphil of עלה; the same verb as v. 4. **LXX:** ἐπέθηκεν τοὺς λύχνους ("he placed the lamps").

**26** And he placed [va-yasem] the altar of [et-mizbach] the gold [ha-zahav] in the tent of [be-ohel] meeting [mo'ed] before [lifnei] the curtain [ha-parokhet].

> **Key terms:** מִזְבַּח הַזָּהָב (mizbach ha-zahav) = "altar of the gold" — the incense altar, positioned before the veil. **LXX:** τὸ θυσιαστήριον τὸ χρυσοῦν ("the golden altar").

**27** And he burned [va-yaqter] upon it [alav] incense of [qetoret] fragrances [sammim], as [ka'asher] YHWH commanded [tsivvah] Moses [et-Mosheh].

> **Key terms:** קְטֹרֶת סַמִּים (qetoret sammim) = "incense of fragrances/spices" — the specially compounded incense (30:34–38). **LXX:** θυμίαμα τῆς συνθέσεως ("incense of the compound").

**28** And he set [va-yasem] the screen of [et-masakh] the entrance [ha-petakh] for the tabernacle [la-mishkan].

> **Key terms:** מָסָךְ הַפֶּתַח (masakh ha-petakh) = "screen of the entrance" — the outer curtain at the tabernacle door. **LXX:** τὸ κατακάλυμμα τῆς θύρας ("the covering of the door").

**29** And the altar of [ve-et mizbach] the burnt offering [ha-olah] he placed [sam] at the entrance of [petakh] the tabernacle of [mishkan] the tent of meeting [ohel mo'ed], and he offered up [va-ya'al] upon it [alav] the burnt offering [et-ha-olah] and the grain offering [ve-et-ha-minkhah], as [ka'asher] YHWH commanded [tsivvah] Moses [et-Mosheh].

> **Key terms:** מִנְחָה (minkhah) = "grain offering/gift-offering" — from a root meaning "to give/present." וַיַּעַל (va-ya'al) = "and he offered up" — hiphil of עלה, the same verb used for lamps; offerings "go up." **LXX:** τὴν ὁλοκαύτωσιν καὶ τὰς θυσίας ("the burnt-offering and the sacrifices").

**30** And he placed [va-yasem] the basin [et-ha-kiyyor] between [bein] the tent of meeting [ohel mo'ed] and between [u-vein] the altar [ha-mizbeakh], and he put [va-yitten] there [shammah] water [mayim] for washing [le-rakhtsah].

> **Key terms:** לְרָחְצָה (le-rakhtsah) = "for washing" — the purpose of the basin: priestly ritual purification. **LXX:** εἰς νίψασθαι ("for washing").

**31** And Moses [Mosheh] and Aaron [ve-Aharon] and his sons [u-vanav] washed [rakhatsu] from it [mimmenu] their hands [et-yedeihem] and their feet [ve-et-ragleihem].

> **Key terms:** רָחֲצוּ (rakhatsu) = "they washed" — the first priestly ablution from the newly erected basin. יָדַיִם ... רַגְלַיִם (yadayim... raglayim) = "hands... feet." **LXX:** ἐνίψαντο ("they washed") — τὰς χεῖρας... τοὺς πόδας ("the hands... the feet").

**32** When they went [be-vo'am] unto [el] the tent of meeting [ohel mo'ed] and when they drew near [u-ve-qorvatam] unto [el] the altar [ha-mizbeakh], they would wash [yirkhiatsu], as [ka'asher] YHWH commanded [tsivvah] Moses [et-Mosheh].

> **Key terms:** בְּבֹאָם (be-vo'am) = "when they went in" — temporal infinitive construct with preposition. יִרְחָצוּ (yirkhiatsu) = "they would wash" — imperfect expressing habitual/repeated action. **LXX:** ἐνίπτοντο ("they were washing") — iterative imperfect.

**33** And he raised up [va-yaqem] the courtyard [et-he-khatser] all around [saviv] the tabernacle [la-mishkan] and the altar [ve-la-mizbeakh], and he set [va-yitten] the screen of [et-masakh] the gate of [sha'ar] the courtyard [he-khatser]. And Moses finished [va-yekhal Mosheh] the work [et-ha-melakhah].

> **Key terms:** וַיְכַל (va-yekhal) = "and he finished" — piel of כלה; the decisive completion verb echoing Genesis 2:1–2, where God "finished" (va-yekhal) the work of creation. מְלָאכָה (melakhah) = "work" — the same word used for God's creation-work. **LXX:** συνετέλεσεν Μωυσῆς τὰ ἔργα ("Moses completed the works").

---

## The Glory of YHWH Fills the Tabernacle (vv. 34–38)

**34** And the cloud [va-yekhas he-anan] covered [va-yekhas] the tent of meeting [et-ohel mo'ed], and the glory of [u-khvod] YHWH filled [male'] the tabernacle [et-ha-mishkan].

> **Key terms:** עָנָן (anan) = "cloud" — the visible sign of the divine Presence. כְּבוֹד יהוה (kevod YHWH) = "the glory of YHWH" — the weight/substance of YHWH's manifest presence. מָלֵא (male') = "filled" — the tabernacle is filled as the cosmos was filled at creation. **LXX:** ἐκάλυψεν ἡ νεφέλη ("the cloud covered") ... δόξης κυρίου ("of the glory of the Lord").

**35** And Moses was not able [ve-lo-yakol Mosheh] to enter [lavo'] unto [el] the tent of meeting [ohel mo'ed], for [ki] the cloud dwelt [shakhan] upon it [alav] and the glory of [u-khvod] YHWH filled [male'] the tabernacle [et-ha-mishkan].

> **Key terms:** שָׁכַן (shakhan) = "dwelt/tabernacled" — the verbal root of mishkan; YHWH "tabernacles" upon the tabernacle. The theology of indwelling presence crystallized in this single verb. **LXX:** ἐπεσκίαζεν ("overshadowed") — a different nuance; the cloud casts shadow over the tent.

**36** And when the cloud went up [u-ve-he'alot he-anan] from upon [me-al] the tabernacle [ha-mishkan], the Sons of Israel [benei Yisra'el] would journey [yis'u] in all [be-khol] their journeys [mas'eihem].

> **Key terms:** בְּהֵעָלוֹת (be-he'alot) = "when [it] went up" — niphal infinitive construct of עלה with temporal bet; the cloud's ascent signals departure. מַסְעֵיהֶם (mas'eihem) = "their journeys/stages" — from נסע ("to pull up/journey"). **LXX:** ἀνέβη ἡ νεφέλη ("the cloud went up") ... ἐξῆραν ("they set out").

**37** And if [ve-im] the cloud did not go up [lo ye'aleh he-anan], then they would not journey [ve-lo yis'u] until the day of [ad-yom] its going up [he'aloto].

> **Key terms:** לֹא יֶעָלֶה (lo ye'aleh) = "it would not go up" — imperfect expressing conditionality. עַד־יוֹם הֵעָלֹתוֹ (ad-yom he'aloto) = "until the day of its going up" — Israel's total dependence on YHWH's timing. **LXX:** οὐκ ἀνέβη... οὐκ ἀνεζεύγνυσαν ("did not go up... they did not decamp").

**38** For [ki] the cloud of [anan] YHWH was upon [al] the tabernacle [ha-mishkan] by day [yomam], and fire [ve-esh] would be [tihyeh] in it [bo] by night [lailah], before the eyes of [le-einei] all [kol] the house of Israel [beit-Yisra'el], in all [be-khol] their journeys [mas'eihem].

> **Key terms:** יוֹמָם (yomam) = "by day"; לַיְלָה (lailah) = "by night" — the twin markers of YHWH's ceaseless presence. בֵּית־יִשְׂרָאֵל (beit-Yisra'el) = "house of Israel" — the final phrase of Exodus names the covenant people as a unified household. **LXX:** πῦρ ἦν ἐπ᾽αὐτῆς νυκτός ("fire was upon it by night") — ἐναντίον παντὸς Ισραηλ ("before all Israel").

---

> **END OF EXODUS (שְׁמוֹת).** The Leningrad Codex marks the conclusion of the book here. The masoretic note at the end of the book records: the total number of verses in Exodus is 1,209; the midpoint is 22:28 [Eng. 22:27]; and the total number of sedarim is 33. The final image of the cloud and fire over the tabernacle — YHWH dwelling in the midst of his people, guiding them through the wilderness — forms the theological bridge to Leviticus (Vayikra), where YHWH speaks from within the tabernacle he has now filled.
